description: Claims-array-shaped input (§8.2 claims_digest canonical input shape): top-level JSON array of two objects with two keys each. Input array element order is deliberately the opposite of any reasonable lexical ordering — element 0 carries 'later'/'zeta', element 1 carries 'earlier'/'alpha' — so a conformant implementation MUST preserve the input array order per §7.5 and per §8.2 ('order preserved as in the proposal'). Each object's keys are provided in non-canonical input order (scope before claim) and MUST be sorted per §7.2. Catches implementations that incorrectly sort array elements (by first-key value, by per-element canonical bytes, or by any other rule) and implementations that skip per-object key sort.
source: hand-verified against docs/canonicalization.md §7.2, §7.5, §7.6, §8.2 and RFC 8785 §3.2.3 by Fabio M. S., 2026-04-20
